description: Official-source evidence pack for the NACE First-Destination Survey. Cards describe what the source covers at the graduating-cohort level (outcome categories and continuing-education framing) with limitations. These are aggregate/cohort-level framings, never individual predictions, and contain no fabricated exact statistics.

items.title: Graduate first-destination outcome categories
items.publisher: National Association of Colleges and Employers (NACE)
items.claim: At the graduating-cohort level, this survey reports where new graduates land shortly after completing a credential, sorted into outcome categories such as employed, continuing education, and still seeking. It tends to describe the mix of post-graduation paths a cohort follows rather than what any one graduate may do.
items.limitations: Coverage is cohort-level and reflects a snapshot taken at a defined point after graduation, so response rates, the institutions that report, and timing can shift the picture; it cannot be read as an individual prediction.

items.title: Continuing-education share of a graduating cohort
items.publisher: National Association of Colleges and Employers (NACE)
items.claim: At the graduating-cohort level, this survey captures the portion of new graduates who report continuing into further education rather than entering the workforce right away. It tends to indicate how common a further-study path is within a cohort, which can vary by field and credential.
items.limitations: Continuing-education figures are aggregate cohort shares shaped by who responded and which programs are represented, so they describe typical patterns for a group and are not a forecast for any single graduate.
